prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, in the third quarter of this most bewildering year, the noble knights of Software-as-a-Service didst vanquish the dragon of Churn, emerging victorious with a retention rate of 92%. 'Tis said that the wise and just King Revenue didst decree a 25% increase in bookings, a bounty most fair and wondrous. Alas, the dark sorcerers of Customer Acquisition Cost didst cast a spell of $120 per head, a sum most grievous and perplexing. By the blessings of the saints, the company's cash hoard didst swell to $15 million, a treasure most fantastical and beyond the understanding of mortal men.
